channel.title: McKinsey Insights & Publications
channel.copyright: Copyright (c) McKinsey & Company. All rights reserved. Reprinted by permission.
channel.generator: Sitecore CMS: http://www.sitecore.net. Sitecore RSS module: McKinsey.RSS, Version=1.3.2.9991, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=null

item.title: Global Banking Annual Review 2025: Why precision, not heft, defines the future of banking
item.description: Banks need to prepare for the next growth curve. Global Banking Annual Review 2025 shows how a targeted approach can help them thrive.

item.title: In conversation: Asia at the geoeconomic crossroads
item.description: Asian governments and companies are striving to adapt to shifting trade winds and escalating strategic competition. Three experts share their insights on various players’ strategies and what may lie ahead.

item.title: Remanufacturing: Turning old parts into new opportunities
item.description: By extending the life cycle of some of their products, companies have been able to cut costs, strengthen supply chains, satisfy customers, and advance their sustainability goals.

item.title: How Nestlé’s CFO sharpens focus across a global portfolio
item.description: Anna Manz shares her learnings on managing geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainty, setting priorities, and the changing nature of the CFO role.

item.title: Brazil’s Financial Evolution: Unveiling the Brazil Stack
item.description: How Pix, Open Finance and blockchain-based rails are transforming the financial landscape and inspiring global innovation

item.title: Closing the women’s health gap: Canada’s $37 billion opportunity
item.description: Closing the women’s health gap in Canada could result in seven more healthy days of life per year for every Canadian woman—and potentially boost the country’s economy by $37 billion annually by 2040.

item.title: Rewiring martech: From cost center to growth engine
item.description: AI is giving leaders the chance for a “re-do” so marketing technology can finally realize its potential.

item.title: Forging a resilient future for California’s homeowners and insurers
item.description: Global lessons and economic insights following the 2025 Los Angeles wildfires could help California build a more stable and resilient homeowners insurance market.

item.title: Operational resilience has become critical. How are banks responding?
item.description: Managing nonfinancial risk requires far more than simply shoring up legacy systems and processes. Leading institutions focus on four actions.

item.title: How to value cyclical companies
item.description: Scenario-based valuation that accounts for multiple potential cycles can yield more accurate forecasts.

item.title: The future of insurance is personal: Insights from Asia’s industry leaders
item.description: What if insurance weren’t just a safety net—but a springboard? Here, AIA, GAIP, and McKinsey explore how insurers can build trust, personalize solutions, and empower communities.

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: Rhyming Hits | No. 255
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: Procurement efficiency: A modern strategy for state and local leaders
item.description: By partnering with their procurement teams, state and local government leaders can improve procurement efficiency using proven practices and leading-edge AI.

item.title: Triple the return: How companies can get more from enterprise tech
item.description: Technology leaders that follow six imperatives—and take a value creation approach to spending—can achieve three times the EBITDA lift from enterprise technology in the agentic age.

item.title: The four seasons of CEO leadership
item.description: CEOs experience four distinct phases of leadership—each with its unique challenges and lessons. Here’s how leaders can thrive at every stage.

item.title: Humanoid robots in the construction industry: A future vision
item.description: Humanoid robots are still at the pilot stage but could emerge as the solution to the construction sector’s productivity problem. How can industry leaders prepare for their entry into the workforce?
item.category: Insights on Engineering, Construction & Building Materials

item.title: The agentic commerce opportunity: How AI agents are ushering in a new era for consumers and merchants
item.description: Agentic AI promises to radically remake the entire shopping experience. Here’s a glimpse into the near future—and what merchants, players, and platforms need to know in order to thrive.

item.title: Nostalgia and opportunity: The resurgence of Europe’s holiday parks
item.description: Europe’s holiday park market, serving a wide variety of domestic travelers, is poised for growth. Five strategies can help operators turn opportunity into reality.

item.title: How foreign direct investment is changing—and why it matters
item.description: Shifts in greenfield FDI are shaking up the industries of the future and rewiring global trade. Geographic distance is easy to understand: look at a map and countries that are eight centimeters apart are obviously more distant than those three centimeters apart. “Geopolitical distance” is a more sophisticated idea: it refers to how closely aligned trade and investment partners are. Japan and the United States, for example, are close geopolitically. Germany and Russia are not, write Olivia White and Tiago Devesa in Foreign Policy.

item.title: Small planes, big changes: The evolving business of regional aviation
item.description: Although the aviation industry has surpassed prepandemic traffic levels, short-haul flights using smaller aircraft have experienced a slower recovery. Are misperceptions holding back the sector?

item.title: New front door to the internet: Winning in the age of AI search
item.description: Half of consumers use AI-powered search today, and it stands to impact $750 billion in revenue by 2028–what is your strategy and activation plan for gen AI engine optimization?

item.title: The AI-centric imperative: Navigating the next software frontier
item.description: With AI-native upstarts redefining speed and scale, software incumbents hoping to remain competitive must fully embrace the new technology to reinvent their value proposition and internal operations.

item.title: Deploying agentic AI with safety and security: A playbook for technology leaders
item.description: Autonomous AI agents present a new world of opportunity—and an array of novel and complex risks and vulnerabilities that require attention and action now.

item.title: How today’s consumers are spending their time and money
item.description: What are the trends shaping consumer sentiment and behavior in 2025—and what are the implications for brands and retailers?

item.title: Unlocking Europe’s €8 billion energy flexibility opportunity
item.description: With growing renewable energy penetration, commercial and industrial companies could improve their energy flexibility mechanisms to stabilize the grid and capture significant value.
item.category: Insights on Electric Power & Natural Gas

item.title: Author Talks: Change management wisdom from Coach’s longtime CEO
item.description: Lew Frankfort pioneered the accessible luxury category, making the Coach bag a must-have for women around the world. His new memoir explains how “magic plus logic” can equal fashion success.

item.title: Smart airports: Clearing the runway for digital takeoff
item.description: Many airports are overdue for a digital transformation. Adopting cutting-edge infrastructure technology could improve traveler experiences, streamline operations, and create substantial value.

item.title: Humanoid robots: Crossing the chasm from concept to commercial reality
item.description: What will it take for humanoid robots to leave pilot purgatory and deliver real value at scale in the workplace?

item.title: Bias Busters: The cognitive brakes on the gen AI accelerator
item.description: Status quo bias and inertia are quiet forces blocking organizations’ progress with gen AI. These moves can overcome them.

item.title: The Big Rethink: An agenda for thriving in the agentic age
item.description: CEOs need a new agenda to help them and their organizations navigate the key questions around AI, particularly agentic AI.

item.title: How today’s developing countries can drive tomorrow’s global economy
item.description: From electric vehicles and generative AI to modular construction and obesity drugs, new industries are transforming economies and societies worldwide. To seize these opportunities, developing countries must create welcoming business environments, attract foreign direct investment, and invest in human capital, write Kweilin Ellingrud, Kevin Russell, and Suhayl Chettih in Project Syndicate.

item.title: Respect above all: Narayana Murthy’s core business belief
item.description: Narayana Murthy, founder of Indian technology services and consulting giant, Infosys, discusses the vital role that respect and compassion play for him in being a successful business leader.

item.title: Deal delays are the new normal. Clean teams are the fix.
item.description: Long waits between signing and close threaten value capture. A new generation of clean teams can safeguard information while ensuring successful integration.

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: Country Club | No. 254
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: Unlocking the power of communication in integrations
item.description: Effective deal communication helps leaders manage change, inspire confidence, and mobilize teams to achieve greater outcomes.

item.title: The building blocks of transformation: A conversation with Udit Batra
item.description: The CEO of Waters Corporation describes how core leadership practices such as deep learning and simple messaging have helped the life sciences company bolster its brand and deliver greater returns.

item.title: Physician perspectives on scaling impact from the CEO seat
item.description: To unlock the full potential of physician leaders, we must first understand what drives them, what they strive for, and what stands in their way.

item.title: Powering cities at the curb: It’s electric and the future of urban EV charging
item.description: Can curbside chargers unlock the future of urban EV adoption while delivering convenience for drivers and easing the burden on the grid? Tiya Gordon of it’s electric and McKinsey’s Shivika Sahdev explore what may be the most practical path to scaling electrification across dense urban neighborhoods.

item.title: Unlocking the growing power of Latino fans: Building a stronger sports economy
item.description: The US sports economy is booming, and Latino fans are leading the charge. Connecting with this multifaceted community can help drive growth opportunities.

item.title: Alex Rodriguez on expanding Latino opportunity and representation in sports
item.description: A-Rod Corp CEO and former Major League Baseball star Alex Rodriguez discusses mentorship, access, and how the sports industry can better serve Latino communities.

item.title: Author Talks: Getting group dynamics right
item.description: Why do some teams thrive while others fail? A researcher of team dynamics and former trumpet player shares how to strike the right chord and build a winning team.

item.title: The economy is walking a tight rope. What could trip it up.
item.description: If you listen to financial markets, the U.S. is on a surefire path of accelerating productivity growth. Some measures of price-to-earnings ratios are higher than they have been in the past two decades. Stock market bulls can point to the productivity promise of artificial intelligence and the 19-fold investment jump since 2010 of the largest tech firms into research and development and capital expenditure. To boot, recent productivity figures look good: 2024 saw double the growth rate of the 2010s, write Olivia White, Rebecca J. Anderson, and Jan Mischke in Barron's.

item.title: Out of balance: What’s next for growth, wealth, and debt?
item.description: Amid rising economic uncertainty, this research takes a long-term view on wealth and growth rooted in government, corporate, and household balance sheets.

item.title: Next best experience: How AI can power every customer interaction
item.description: By harnessing AI and gen AI to create the next best customer experience, front-runners could generate value for customers and themselves through increased conversion, retention, and upselling.

item.title: Europe is out of shape, but a surge of productive investment could set it right
item.description: Europe must boost investment. Its productivity and competitiveness depend on it – as do its future wealth and debt sustainability. Europe, like much of the rest of the world, still faces a bloated balance sheet. Its assets have ballooned faster than its productive output for more than two decades now. And as the world’s wealth grew to an unprecedented $600tn this year, the eurozone’s wealth also climbed to $135tn – 6.6 times its GDP, write Tiago Devesa, Jan Mischke, and Sylvain Johansson in Friends of Europe.

item.title: What sets the world’s best leaders apart
item.description: A new book reveals what makes some CEOs truly iconic—as well as what aspiring leaders stand to learn from their experiences.

item.title: How CEOs can play the long game in a shortsighted world
item.description: FCLTGlobal’s Sarah Keohane Williamson discusses the importance of resisting short-termism, rethinking investor relations, and reframing incentives.

item.title: Upgrading corporate affairs for a new geopolitical era
item.description: Geopolitical tensions and economic competition are raising the demands on corporate-affairs teams. Here are five ways they can refresh their playbooks to more effectively manage geoeconomic complexity.

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: Head Strong | No. 253
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: What’s next for climate tech?
item.description: How 80 percent of emission reductions can also deliver energy security.
